mn46:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn46:10.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*], one who knows, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understands it as it truly is:
mn46:10.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:10.3: Knowing that, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understanding it as it truly is, he does not engage in it, but avoids it.
mn46:10.4: For him, while not engaging in it and while avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* decline, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* increase.
mn46:10.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for a discerning person.
mn46:11.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*], one who knows, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understands it as it truly is:
mn46:11.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:11.3: Knowing that, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understanding it as it truly is, he does not engage in it, but avoids it.
mn46:11.4: For him, while not engaging in it and while avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* decline, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* increase.
mn46:11.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for a discerning person.
mn46:12.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*], one who knows, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understands it as it truly is:
mn46:12.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:12.3: Knowing that, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understanding it as it truly is, he engages in it and does not avoid it.
mn46:12.4: For him, while engaging in it and not avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* decline, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* increase.
mn46:12.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for a discerning person.
mn46:13.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*], one who knows, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understands it as it truly is:
mn46:13.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:13.3: Knowing that, under the sway of knowledge [*vijjāgato*], understanding it as it truly is, he engages in it and does not avoid it.
mn46:13.4: For him, while engaging in it and not avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* decline, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* increase.
mn46:13.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for a discerning person.
mn46:14.1: And what, bhikkhus, is the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*]?
mn46:14.10: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he has a mind of ill will [*byāpannacitto*], and because of ill will he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.11: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he has wrong view, and because of wrong view he experiences *dukkha* and sadness.
mn46:14.12: With the breakup of the body, after death, he is reborn in a state of loss, a bad destination, downfall, hell.
mn46:14.13: This is called, bhikkhus, the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*].
mn46:14.2: Here, bhikkhus, someone, together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, kills living beings, and because of killing living beings he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.3: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he takes what is not given, and because of taking what is not given he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.4: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he commits sexual misconduct, and because of sexual misconduct he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.5: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he lies, and because of lying he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.6: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he speaks divisively, and because of divisive speech he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.7: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he speaks harshly, and because of harsh speech he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.8: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he engages in idle chatter, and because of idle chatter he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:14.9: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he is covetous, and because of covetousness he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:15.1: And what, bhikkhus, is the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*]?
mn46:15.10: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he has a mind of ill will [*byāpannacitto*], and because of ill will he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.11: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he has wrong view, and because of wrong view he experiences *sukha* and gladness.
mn46:15.12: With the breakup of the body, after death, he is reborn in a state of loss, a bad destination, downfall, hell.
mn46:15.13: This is called, bhikkhus, the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*].
mn46:15.2: Here, bhikkhus, someone, together with *sukha* and together with gladness, kills living beings, and because of killing living beings he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.3: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he takes what is not given, and because of taking what is not given he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.4: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he commits sexual misconduct, and because of sexual misconduct he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.5: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he lies, and because of lying he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.6: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he speaks divisively, and because of divisive speech he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.7: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he speaks harshly, and because of harsh speech he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.8: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he engages in idle chatter, and because of idle chatter he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:15.9: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he is covetous, and because of covetousness he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:16.1: And what, bhikkhus, is the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*]?
mn46:16.10: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he has a mind free of ill will [*abyāpannacitto*], and because of non-ill will he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.11: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he has right view, and because of right view he experiences *dukkha* and sadness.
mn46:16.12: With the breakup of the body, after death, he is reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.
mn46:16.13: This is called, bhikkhus, the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].
mn46:16.2: Here, bhikkhus, someone, together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, abstains from killing living beings, and because of abstaining from killing living beings he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.3: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he abstains from taking what is not given, and because of abstaining from taking what is not given he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.4: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he abstains from sexual misconduct, and because of abstaining from sexual misconduct he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.5: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he abstains from lying, and because of abstaining from lying he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.6: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he abstains from divisive speech, and because of abstaining from divisive speech he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.7: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he abstains from harsh speech, and because of abstaining from harsh speech he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.8: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he abstains from idle chatter, and because of abstaining from idle chatter he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:16.9: together with *dukkha* and together with sadness, he is non-covetous, and because of non-covetousness he experiences *dukkha* and sadness;
mn46:17.1: And what, bhikkhus, is the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*]?
mn46:17.10: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he has a mind free of ill will [*abyāpannacitto*], and because of non-ill will he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.11: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he has right view, and because of right view he experiences *sukha* and gladness.
mn46:17.12: With the breakup of the body, after death, he is reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.
mn46:17.13: This is called, bhikkhus, the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].
mn46:17.14: These, bhikkhus, are the four ways of taking up *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānāni*].
mn46:17.2: Here, bhikkhus, someone, together with *sukha* and together with gladness, abstains from killing living beings, and because of abstaining from killing living beings he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.3: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he abstains from taking what is not given, and because of abstaining from taking what is not given he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.4: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he abstains from sexual misconduct, and because of abstaining from sexual misconduct he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.5: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he abstains from lying, and because of abstaining from lying he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.6: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he abstains from divisive speech, and because of abstaining from divisive speech he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.7: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he abstains from harsh speech, and because of abstaining from harsh speech he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.8: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he abstains from idle chatter, and because of abstaining from idle chatter he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:17.9: together with *sukha* and together with gladness, he is non-covetous, and because of non-covetousness he experiences *sukha* and gladness;
mn46:18.1: “Suppose, bhikkhus, there were a bitter bottle-gourd mixed with poison.
mn46:18.2: Then a man might come along wanting to live, not wanting to die, wanting *sukha*, recoiling from *dukkha*.
mn46:18.4: ‘Come, man, this bitter bottle-gourd is mixed with poison.
mn46:18.6: As you drink it, it will not please you in color, smell, or taste; but after drinking it, you will either meet death or suffering like death.’
mn46:18.7: Without reflecting, he would drink it and would not discard it.
mn46:18.8: As he drank it, it would not please him in color, smell, or taste; and after drinking it, he would either meet death or suffering like death.
mn46:18.9: I say that this taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*] is similar to that.
mn46:19.1: Suppose, bhikkhus, there were a drinking cup, perfect in color, perfect in smell, perfect in taste.
mn46:19.10: As he drank it, it would please him in color, smell, and taste; and after drinking it, he would either meet death or suffering like death.
mn46:19.11: I say that this taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*] is similar to that.
mn46:19.2: But it was mixed with poison.
mn46:19.3: Then a man might come along wanting to live, not wanting to die, wanting *sukha*, recoiling from *dukkha*.
mn46:19.5: ‘Come, man, this drinking cup is perfect in color, perfect in smell, perfect in taste.
mn46:19.6: But it is mixed with poison.
mn46:19.8: As you drink it, it will please you in color, smell, and taste; but after drinking it, you will either meet death or suffering like death.’
mn46:19.9: Without reflecting, he would drink it and would not discard it.
mn46:2.1: “For the most part, bhikkhus, beings want thus, desire thus, intend thus:
mn46:2.2: ‘Oh, if only unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* would decline, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* would increase.’
mn46:2.3: But for those beings who want thus, desire thus, and intend thus, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* increase, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* decline.
mn46:2.4: What cause do you understand there for this, bhikkhus?”
mn46:2.5: “Our *dhammas*, *bhante*, are rooted in the *Bhagavā*, guided by the *Bhagavā*, with the *Bhagavā* as refuge. It would be good, *bhante*, if the meaning of this statement would become clear from the *Bhagavā* himself. Having heard it from the *Bhagavā*, the *bhikkhus* will remember it.”
mn46:2.6: “Then listen, bhikkhus, and attend closely. I will speak.”
mn46:20.1: Suppose, bhikkhus, there were stale urine mixed with various medicines.
mn46:20.2: Then a man suffering from jaundice might come along.
mn46:20.4: ‘Come, man, this stale urine is mixed with various medicines. If you wish, drink it.
mn46:20.5: As you drink it, it will not please you in color, smell, or taste; but after drinking it, you will be well.’
mn46:20.6: After reflecting, he would drink it and would not discard it.
mn46:20.7: As he drank it, it would not please him in color, smell, or taste; but after drinking it, he would be well.
mn46:20.8: I say that this taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*] is similar to that.
mn46:21.1: Suppose, bhikkhus, curd, honey, ghee, and molasses were all mixed together.
mn46:21.2: Then a man suffering from bloody dysentery might come along.
mn46:21.4: ‘Come, man, this curd, honey, ghee, and molasses are all mixed together. If you wish, drink it.
mn46:21.5: As you drink it, it will please you in color, smell, and taste; and after drinking it, you will be well.’
mn46:21.6: After reflecting, he would drink it and would not discard it.
mn46:21.7: As he drank it, it would please him in color, smell, and taste; and after drinking it, he would be well.
mn46:21.8: I say that this taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*] is similar to that.
mn46:22.1: Just as, bhikkhus, in the last month of the rains, in the autumn season, when the sky is clear and cloudless, the sun, rising into the sky, having dispelled all darkness from space, shines and blazes and radiates;
mn46:22.2: just so, bhikkhus, this taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*], having outshone the doctrines of other ordinary ascetics and brahmins, shines and blazes and radiates.”
mn46:22.4: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in the *Bhagavā*’s words.
mn46:22.5: The Greater Taking Up of *Dhammas* is finished, the sixth.
mn46:3.1: “Here, bhikkhus, an uninstructed ordinary person, who has not seen the noble ones, who is unskilled in the noble *dhamma* [*ariyadhammassa*], untrained in the noble *dhamma* [*ariyadhamme*], who has not seen true persons, who is unskilled in the true persons’ *dhamma* [*sappurisadhammassa*], untrained in the true persons’ *dhamma* [*sappurisadhamme*],
mn46:3.2: does not know the *dhammas* to be engaged in, does not know the *dhammas* not to be engaged in, does not know the *dhammas* to be cultivated, does not know the *dhammas* not to be cultivated.
mn46:3.3: Not knowing the *dhammas* to be engaged in, not knowing the *dhammas* not to be engaged in, not knowing the *dhammas* to be cultivated, not knowing the *dhammas* not to be cultivated, he engages in the *dhammas* not to be engaged in and does not engage in the *dhammas* to be engaged in; he cultivates the *dhammas* not to be cultivated and does not cultivate the *dhammas* to be cultivated.
mn46:3.4: For him, while engaging in the *dhammas* not to be engaged in and not engaging in the *dhammas* to be engaged in, while cultivating the *dhammas* not to be cultivated and not cultivating the *dhammas* to be cultivated, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* increase, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* decline.
mn46:3.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for an undiscerning person.
mn46:4.1: But, bhikkhus, an instructed noble disciple, who has seen the noble ones, who is skilled in the noble *dhamma* [*ariyadhammassa*], well trained in the noble *dhamma* [*ariyadhamme*], who has seen true persons, who is skilled in the true persons’ *dhamma* [*sappurisadhammassa*], well trained in the true persons’ *dhamma* [*sappurisadhamme*],
mn46:4.2: knows the *dhammas* to be engaged in, knows the *dhammas* not to be engaged in, knows the *dhammas* to be cultivated, knows the *dhammas* not to be cultivated.
mn46:4.3: Knowing the *dhammas* to be engaged in, knowing the *dhammas* not to be engaged in, knowing the *dhammas* to be cultivated, knowing the *dhammas* not to be cultivated, he does not engage in the *dhammas* not to be engaged in and does engage in the *dhammas* to be engaged in; he does not cultivate the *dhammas* not to be cultivated and does cultivate the *dhammas* to be cultivated.
mn46:4.4: For him, while not engaging in the *dhammas* not to be engaged in and engaging in the *dhammas* to be engaged in, while not cultivating the *dhammas* not to be cultivated and cultivating the *dhammas* to be cultivated, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* decline, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* increase.
mn46:4.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for a discerning person.
mn46:5.1: There are these four ways of taking up *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānāni*], bhikkhus.
mn46:5.3: There is, bhikkhus, a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*];
mn46:5.4: there is, bhikkhus, a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*];
mn46:5.5: there is, bhikkhus, a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*];
mn46:5.6: there is, bhikkhus, a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].
mn46:6.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*], one who does not know, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], does not understand it as it truly is:
mn46:6.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:6.3: Not knowing that, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], not understanding it as it truly is, he engages in it and does not avoid it.
mn46:6.4: For him, while engaging in it and not avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* increase, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* decline.
mn46:6.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for an undiscerning person.
mn46:7.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*], one who does not know, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], does not understand it as it truly is:
mn46:7.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *dukkha* result [*dukkhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:7.3: Not knowing that, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], not understanding it as it truly is, he engages in it and does not avoid it.
mn46:7.4: For him, while engaging in it and not avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* increase, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* decline.
mn46:7.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for an undiscerning person.
mn46:8.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*], one who does not know, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], does not understand it as it truly is:
mn46:8.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *dukkha* [*paccuppannadukkhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:8.3: Not knowing that, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], not understanding it as it truly is, he does not engage in it, but avoids it.
mn46:8.4: For him, while not engaging in it and while avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* increase, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* decline.
mn46:8.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for an undiscerning person.
mn46:9.1: Therein, bhikkhus, as for the taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*], one who does not know, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], does not understand it as it truly is:
mn46:9.2: ‘This is a taking up of *dhammas* [*dhammasamādānaṁ*] with present *sukha* [*paccuppannasukhaṁ*] and future *sukha* result [*sukhavipākaṁ*].’
mn46:9.3: Not knowing that, under the sway of blindness [*avijjāgato*], not understanding it as it truly is, he does not engage in it, but avoids it.
mn46:9.4: For him, while not engaging in it and while avoiding it, unwanted, unloved, disagreeable *dhammas* increase, and wanted, loved, agreeable *dhammas* decline.
mn46:9.6: Because that is how it is, bhikkhus, for an undiscerning person.
